examples.title: Recursive Binary Tree Count using Catalan Recurrence
examples.description: Algorithm to compute number of binary trees with n nodes using dynamic programming based on Catalan recurrence T(n) = Σ(i=0 to n-1) T(i)T(n-1-i)
examples.code:
FUNCTION CountBinaryTrees(n):
  // Returns the nth Catalan number Cₙ
  IF n <= 1 THEN
    RETURN 1
  ENDIF
  
  // Using dynamic programming for efficiency
  CREATE ARRAY dp[0..n]
  SET dp[0] = 1
  SET dp[1] = 1
  
  FOR i FROM 2 TO n:
    SET dp[i] = 0
    FOR j FROM 0 TO i-1:
      dp[i] = dp[i] + dp[j] * dp[i-1-j]
    ENDFOR
  ENDFOR
  
  RETURN dp[n]
END FUNCTION

// Example: CountBinaryTrees(4) returns 14
examples.explanation: The algorithm implements the Catalan recurrence relation using dynamic programming. The outer loop computes each Cₙ, and the inner loop performs convolution to combine smaller subtree counts. Time complexity: O(n²) for computing Cₙ. Space complexity: O(n). This is much faster than naive recursion which has exponential time complexity.

examples.title: Generate All Binary Tree Structures
examples.description: Recursive algorithm to enumerate all distinct binary tree structures with n nodes
examples.code:
FUNCTION GenerateAllTrees(n):
  // Returns list of all binary tree structures with n nodes
  IF n == 0 THEN
    RETURN [NULL]  // Empty tree
  ENDIF
  IF n == 1 THEN
    RETURN [new Node(null, null)]  // Single node tree
  ENDIF
  
  SET result = EMPTY LIST
  
  // For each possible left subtree size i
  FOR leftSize FROM 0 TO n-1:
    rightSize = n - 1 - leftSize
    
    leftTrees = GenerateAllTrees(leftSize)
    rightTrees = GenerateAllTrees(rightSize)
    
    // Combine each left tree with each right tree
    FOR EACH lt IN leftTrees:
      FOR EACH rt IN rightTrees:
        root = new Node(lt, rt)
        ADD root TO result
      ENDFOR
    ENDFOR
  ENDFOR
  
  RETURN result
END FUNCTION

// To generate and count: trees = GenerateAllTrees(4)
// Count will be 14 = C₄
examples.explanation: This algorithm generates all Cₙ binary tree structures through recursive decomposition. For n nodes, it iterates through all possible splits (leftSize from 0 to n-1), generates all left and right subtrees, and combines them with a root node. The total output size is Θ(Cₙ × n) representing all trees. Time complexity is O(Cₙ × n) which is optimal since we must output all trees. Space complexity is O(n) for recursion stack plus O(Cₙ × n) for storing all generated trees.

examples.title: Verify Binary Tree Properties
examples.description: Algorithm to verify whether a given binary tree is full, complete, or perfect
examples.code:
FUNCTION CheckTreeType(root):
  SET result = {}
  
  // Count nodes and check properties
  nodeCount = CountNodes(root)
  height = GetHeight(root)
  
  // Check if perfect: all leaves at same level
  result.isPerfect = CheckPerfect(root, 0, height)
  
  // Check if full: every node has 0 or 2 children
  result.isFull = CheckFull(root)
  
  // Check if complete: all levels filled except possibly last
  result.isComplete = CheckComplete(root)
  
  RETURN result
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION CheckPerfect(node, level, height):
  IF node == NULL THEN
    RETURN TRUE
  ENDIF
  IF level == height THEN
    // At deepest level, must be leaf
    RETURN (node.left == NULL AND node.right == NULL)
  ENDIF
  // Internal nodes must have both children
  IF node.left == NULL OR node.right == NULL THEN
    RETURN FALSE
  ENDIF
  RETURN CheckPerfect(node.left, level+1, height) AND
         CheckPerfect(node.right, level+1, height)
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION CheckFull(node):
  IF node == NULL THEN
    RETURN TRUE
  ENDIF
  IF (node.left == NULL AND node.right != NULL) OR
     (node.left != NULL AND node.right == NULL) THEN
    RETURN FALSE
  ENDIF
  RETURN CheckFull(node.left) AND CheckFull(node.right)
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION CheckComplete(node):
  // Use level-order traversal with flag for missing node
  IF node == NULL THEN
    RETURN TRUE
  ENDIF
  SET queue = [node]
  SET foundNull = FALSE
  
  WHILE queue is not empty:
    temp = queue.dequeue()
    IF temp == NULL THEN
      foundNull = TRUE
    ELSE IF foundNull == TRUE THEN
      RETURN FALSE  // Non-null after null violates completeness
    ELSE:
      queue.enqueue(temp.left)
      queue.enqueue(temp.right)
    ENDIF
  ENDWHILE
  
  RETURN TRUE
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates verification of three binary tree properties. CheckPerfect uses recursive traversal comparing leaf depths. CheckFull recursively ensures no node has exactly one child. CheckComplete uses BFS with a flag - once a null child is found, all subsequent nodes must also be null. Time complexities: CheckPerfect O(n), CheckFull O(n), CheckComplete O(n). Space complexities: CheckPerfect/Full O(h), CheckComplete O(n) for queue.
